The theme of the weekend will be warm, with temperatures topping out close to record levels by Sunday. It will feel more like mid-June than late March. In the meantime, a disturbance aloft coming through late today and tonight could be enough to kick off a couple of showers or thundershowers (most places stay rain-free), also reminiscent of summer. We finish out the weekend very nice, but then temperatures tumble back closer to seasonal norms this week.

Setting up for Summer Preview Sunday

Last Updated Sat March 21 at 10:51AM by Alan Simmons

The theme of the weekend will be warm, with temperatures topping out close to record levels by Sunday. It will feel more like mid-June than late March. In the meantime, a disturbance aloft coming through late today and tonight could be enough to kick off a couple of showers or thundershowers (most places stay rain-free), also reminiscent of summer. We finish out the weekend very nice, but then temperatures tumble back closer to seasonal norms this week.

The "Mix and Match" Special Offer continues on all the Snowman O'Mometer Pint Glass and Mug (or you can long for a 5 Golfball Day with that pint glass as well). Buy one, and get another drinkware item at 40% off.

Forecast Discussion

Warmer temperatures today are accompanied by a subtle increase in low level moisture. An upper level disturbance passing overhead will interact with that moisture to kick off a few stray showers or thundershowers very late in the day and into tonight. Don't cancel your plans; most places should be rain-free.

The summer preview continues into Sunday with lower rain chances, more sunshine, and very warm temperatures for late March. Hope you get a chance to enjoy it.

Sunday night, a cold front comes through and that puts temperatures a lot closer to where they usually are for this time of year. There could be a light shower or two late Sunday night or Monday morning as the cooler air moves in, especially along the western escarpment.

High pressure crossing the lower Great Lakes region early this week should keep the weather settled until midweek, when a weak southern stream impulse could bring us a little light rain or drizzle Wednesday into Wednesday night. Thursday features more sunshine and a brief mild-up once again, before another front drops in from the north to bring some showers and cooler temperatures to finish out the work week.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Monday Message for March 16, 2026

Quiet weather returns for western North Carolina and Southwest Virginia after a hyper-active Monday around the region. Severe weather is followed by a sharp cold shot and mountain snow shower episode, then we moderate nicely for late week through the weekend.

Mar 16, 2026
Mid-March Madness

A strong cold front crosses the Southern Appalachians Monday morning and moves into the Foothills Monday afternoon.

Rain and thunderstorms accompany the frontal passage, and severe thunderstorms are ...

Mar 16, 2026
Severe Weather Season Around The Corner

As our March weather has become more changeable and volatile, it's a reminder that severe weather season is almost upon us.

Last week was Severe Weather Awareness Week in North Carolina; This week is Virginia's turn.

While western North Carolina and Southwest Virginia may have less overall severe weather than our surrounding neighbors, we still have our fair share, and it's a great time to review your severe weather emergency/preparedeness procedures ahead of time.
